sourceMovie.title: The Core
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Core works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Core clicked for you, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Core clicked for you, The Day After Tomorrow (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Core?
faq.answer: Try Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials, The Day After Tomorrow, Armageddon—each shares DNA with The Core while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Core?
faq.answer: The Core (2003) is best described as Science Fiction, Adventure, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Core worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
